insights.message: bond0: slave enp6s0f3u1 is a USB NIC — USB adapters are less reliable than PCIe NICs for production bonding (can be unplugged, USB bus is a single point of failure)
hints: note: consider replacing with a PCIe or onboard NIC for production HA

insights.message: transparent huge pages mode is 'always' — may cause latency spikes for database workloads
hints: to check:   if running MySQL/PostgreSQL/Redis/MongoDB, set to 'madvise' or 'never'
to persist: add to /etc/rc.local or a systemd service

insights.message: CPU governor is 'powersave' — CPU running at 1102 MHz (max 4465 MHz), performance limited
hints: to persist: add to /etc/rc.local or use tuned profile 'throughput-performance'
note: 'schedutil' or 'ondemand' are also acceptable for variable workloads

insights.message: vm.swappiness=60 is high for a server (recommended: ≤ 30; production servers typically use 10)

details.title: Sysctl: current vs recommended
rows: lower for k8s/database; 60 for general workloads

insights.message: net.core.rmem_max=212992 is low (recommended: ≥ 4MB for modern network throughput)
SELinux enforcing — if services fail unexpectedly, dontaudit rules may suppress denials silently
hints: to expose hidden denials: semodule -DB  (disables dontaudit rules)
to re-enable dontaudit:   semodule -B   (run after debugging)
to check for suppressed:  ausearch -m avc -ts recent --raw | wc -l

insights.message: inactive volume group ubuntu-vg is 11% full — no LVs mounted (old OS partition?)
hints: note: this VG has no mounted LVs on this OS — likely a leftover from a previous install

insights.message: journald SyncIntervalSec is high (default 5min) — final log lines from a crashing process may be lost
hints: note:   lower sync interval increases disk I/O but ensures crash logs are preserved

insights.message: 15 error(s) logged in the last hour — check if expected
hints: to inspect: journalctl -p err --since '1 hour ago' --no-pager

insights.message: SSH allows password authentication — key-based auth recommended
hints: to fix: set PasswordAuthentication no in /etc/ssh/sshd_config

insights.message: SSH LoginGraceTime is 120s — recommend ≤60s to limit unauthenticated connection window
SSH X11Forwarding enabled — unnecessary on servers, increases attack surface
hints: to fix: set X11Forwarding no in /etc/ssh/sshd_config
note: only needed if users require GUI applications over SSH

insights.message: SSH AgentForwarding enabled — if this server is compromised, agent keys on your laptop can be stolen
hints: to fix: set AllowAgentForwarding no in /etc/ssh/sshd_config
note: use ssh -A explicitly when you need forwarding, rather than leaving it on globally

insights.message: SSH idle timeout not set — sessions stay open indefinitely (set ClientAliveInterval)
hints: note: this disconnects idle sessions after 300s × 3 = 15 minutes

insights.message: SSH weak MAC(s) enabled (sshd -T (effective config)): hmac-sha1
hints: note: hmac-sha1 uses SHA-1 which is cryptographically broken

insights.message: supportconfig available but never run — collect before opening SUSE support ticket
password never expires for human account(s): andrei
hints: to fix all: awk -F: '($3>=1000 && $3<65534){print $1}' /etc/passwd | xargs -I{} chage -M 90 {}
note: CIS benchmark recommends maximum password age ≤ 365 days

insights.message: bond bond0: slave enp6s0f3u1 is a USB NIC — less reliable than PCIe for production bonding
hints: note: USB adapters can be accidentally unplugged; USB bus is a single point of failure
note: consider replacing with a PCIe or onboard NIC for production HA
